title: European imaging companies step in to fill warzone gap
summary: European Earth-observation firms are stepping in to provide satellite imagery of conflict zones like Iran and the Gulf region after U.S. companies reduced their coverage. This shift highlights Europe's growing role in addressing gaps in geospatial intelligence amid ongoing conflicts.

title: Four NASA payloads to fly on Astrolab’s first lunar rover
summary: Astrolab's first lunar rover will carry four NASA payloads on a mission scheduled for later this year. This marks a significant collaboration between Astrolab and NASA in lunar exploration.

title: Inside Golden Dome’s push to court commercial tech firms and investors
summary: Pentagon officials are promoting Golden Dome as a modern technology platform rather than a traditional weapons system to attract commercial space firms, investors, and software companies. The initiative aims to leverage commercial innovation for missile defense.

title: Tomorrow.io adds $35 million to DeepSky funding round
summary: Weather intelligence provider Tomorrow.io raised an additional $35 million for its DeepSky funding round, bringing the total to $210 million to advance its next-generation atmospheric data constellation.

title: House bill restores funding for TraCSS
summary: A House appropriations bill aims to restore funding for the Traffic Coordination System for Space (TraCSS), reversing an administration decision to halt its development. The bill focuses on maintaining civil space traffic management capabilities.

title: New CSF Report Sees Up To 7,000+ Satellites Launched Annually By Mid 2030’s, Highlights The Challenges With US Launch Infrastructure
summary: A new report by the Commercial Space Federation (CSF) and Rational Futures (RF) projects up to 7,000+ satellites launched annually by the mid-2030s, while highlighting significant challenges with U.S. launch infrastructure. The report, titled 'SCRUBBED: America’s Launch Capacity Challenge,' emphasizes the strain on current launch capabilities.

title: The next war will be won — or lost — in orbit
summary: The conflict in Ukraine has highlighted the critical role of space in modern warfare, prompting Western Europe to increase defense spending. However, building an effective defense ecosystem takes years, even with significant investment.

title: Zenk Space raises $26 million, targets June debut launch
summary: Zenk Space, a Chinese company, has raised 180 million yuan for its first orbital launch attempt with the Zhihang-1 kerolox rocket, scheduled for June in Kourou, French Guiana.

title: NASA releases final RFP for Mars communications orbiter
summary: NASA issued the final request for proposals for a Mars telecommunications orbiter, restricting eligible bidders. The move aims to secure reliable communications infrastructure for future Mars missions.

title: York Space defends growth strategy as SDA programs are reshaped
summary: York Space Systems is defending its growth strategy amid a reshaping of Pentagon programs that have driven its expansion. The company faces challenges as key programs undergo significant reorganization.

title: Why bringing back 'The Mandalorian and Grogu' isn't the way
summary: The article argues that 'The Mandalorian and Grogu' should not have been released theatrically and questions its existence. It criticizes the theatrical release decision.

title: 'Voidverse' is a seriously weird space fantasy novel from 'The OA's' Damien Ober (interview)
summary: Voidverse is a space fantasy novel authored by Damien Ober, known for The OA. The interview highlights the need for characters and prose to align with the novel's unique atmosphere and setting.

title: What time is SpaceX's Starship V3 launch on May 20? (Starship Flight 12 timeline)
summary: SpaceX is scheduled to launch its first Starship V3 vehicle on Flight 12 on May 20. The event marks a significant milestone in the development of the Starship program.

title: Watch Vega C rocket launch European-Chinese space weather satellite to orbit tonight
summary: A Vega C rocket is set to launch the European-Chinese SMILE space weather mission to orbit tonight (May 18), with live coverage available. This mission aims to study space weather and its effects.

title: This amazing NASA video shows the exact moment the Artemis 2 Orion capsule broke free of its service module, and we can't stop watching it
summary: A NASA video captures the moment the Artemis 2 Orion capsule separated from its service module before re-entering Earth's atmosphere on April 10, 2026. The footage highlights a critical phase of the mission.

title: SpaceX Starship Flight 12 launch updates: 1st-ever Starship V3 test gets new launch date
summary: SpaceX has scheduled the launch of its Starship V3 megarocket for May 20. This marks the first test flight of the upgraded Starship design.

title: Artemis 2 moon astronauts snap gorgeous shot of swirling stars | Space photo of the day for May 18, 2026
summary: The Artemis 2 astronauts captured a stunning space photo of swirling stars from the Orion capsule. This image highlights the mission's progress toward the moon.

title: Manhattanhenge 2026 is coming this month: Here's when and where to see the sunset spectacle
summary: Manhattanhenge 2026 will occur this month, offering a unique spectacle where the sunset aligns perfectly with Manhattan's street grid. This event, similar to the famous Stonehenge alignment, is a popular skywatching phenomenon in New York City.

title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4893-4899: Drilling at Campo Marte and a Visit From the Psyche Spacecraft
summary: NASA's Curiosity rover is preparing to drill at a new target named Campo Marte, representing the same geologic stratum as the previously freed 'Atacama' block. The mission also involves a visit from the Psyche spacecraft during this period.

title: Johnson Photographers Honored for Award-Winning Portraits
summary: Three photographers at NASA's Johnson Space Center were honored for their award-winning portraits at the 2025 NASA Imagery Experts Program Annual Awards. Their work highlights visual storytelling and collaboration within the space agency.

title: NASA’s MAVEN Makes 1st Discovery of Atmospheric Effect at Mars
summary: NASA's MAVEN mission discovered a new atmospheric phenomenon on Mars, resembling a known effect in Earth's magnetosphere. The finding was made using instruments aboard the spacecraft in December 2023.

title: NASA Selects Next Class of Space Health Postdoctoral Fellows
summary: NASA's Translational Research Institute for Space Health selected two postdoctoral fellows to research space food systems and astronaut eye health. The fellows will begin their projects in May under TRISH's fellowship program.

title: Beacon of Light
summary: NASA's James Webb Space Telescope captured an image of galaxy M77's bright core on May 7, 2026, revealing intense light caused by gas heated as it orbits a central black hole.

title: NASA Science, Cargo Launch on 34th SpaceX Resupply Mission to Station
summary: NASA's 34th SpaceX resupply mission launched to the International Space Station with new scientific experiments aboard a Falcon 9 rocket from Cape Canaveral. The spacecraft carried nearly 6,500 pounds of cargo.

title: Curiosity Shakes Loose a Pesky Rock
summary: NASA's Curiosity Mars rover successfully detached a rock that had lodged in its drill after several days of troubleshooting. The rover drilled a sample from the rock on April 25, 2026, and later removed it on May 1.

title: Hubble Sights Galaxy in Transition
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of a galaxy with a bright center and hints of a spiral structure, though it lacks distinct spiral arms. The galaxy features reddish-brown dust filaments and clumps, with light from distant galaxies visible in the background.

title: Picturing Earth in a New Light
summary: A recent analysis has identified areas where artificial light at night has increased and decreased, providing new insights into global lighting trends. The study highlights changes in nighttime illumination across different regions.

title: NASA Draws on Industry for Mars Telecommunications Network
summary: NASA has issued a Request for Proposal (RFP) to collaborate with industry for the Mars Telecommunications Network, aiming to provide high-bandwidth communications for future Mars missions. The network will support surface, orbital, and human exploration by using advanced Mars telecommunications orbiters.

title: Vega C set to launch SMILE to study Earth’s magnetosphere
summary: ESA's SMILE mission is set to launch aboard Arianespace's Vega C rocket to study Earth's magnetosphere. The mission aims to investigate the interaction between the solar wind and Earth's magnetic field.

title: Super Heavy Block 3 the Booster of the Future
summary: The Super Heavy Block 3 booster, the first stage of SpaceX's Starship, has undergone multiple iterations. This version represents a significant advancement in booster technology for future missions.

title: Artemis III deep into processing and planning for key HLS test
summary: NASA is actively processing and planning Artemis III, a significant crewed mission to low Earth orbit. The mission includes key tests for the Human Landing System (HLS).

title: Flight 12 readies for the debut SpaceX’s next Ship evolution
summary: SpaceX is preparing for Flight 12, marking the debut of the next evolution of Starship's second stage. This follows multiple iterations since the early test flights of SN8.

title: Rocket Lab signs new launch contracts and acquires robotics company during Q1 2026
summary: Rocket Lab achieved record-breaking performance in Q1 2026 by signing new launch contracts and acquiring a robotics company. The company surpassed all its 2025 milestones during this period.

title: SpaceX’s new Starship pad readies for first launch
summary: SpaceX has completed construction of a new launch pad for its Starship rocket. The pad is now preparing for its first launch.

title: SpaceX CRS-34 launches to ISS on third attempt
summary: SpaceX successfully launched the CRS-34 mission to the International Space Station (ISS) on its third attempt. The launch follows the docking of Progress MS-34 to the ISS.

title: Launch Preview: SpaceX and Chinese missions fill busy launch manifest
summary: SpaceX and China are set to conduct nine launches in the week of May 11, marking a busy period for space missions. The launches span various activities in the United States and China.

title: Full Stack Flight 12 completes WDR
summary: Full Stack Flight 12 completed a Wet Dress Rehearsal (WDR) after Booster 19 successfully conducted a 33-engine static fire test on May 7, 2026. The milestone paves the way for future launch preparations.

title: China prepares cargo, crew, and deep space missions, as commercial sector steps towards reusability
summary: China is preparing to launch cargo and crew missions to its Tiangong space station while advancing its deep space exploration goals. The country's commercial sector is also making strides toward reusability in space missions.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over its 50-year history. The publication celebrates significant milestones in space exploration and technology.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are helping the European Space Agency test and validate materials for spacecraft to ensure they withstand the harsh conditions of space. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section relies on innovative ideas from these young minds to support ESA's missions.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance both technical and educational aspects of space missions.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: The article discusses a critical test for spacecraft parachutes, referred to as 'the great parachute bake-out.' This test ensures the parachutes' functionality under extreme conditions.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA's ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission is preparing a 35-meter parachute for Mars by sterilizing it to prevent microbial contamination. The parachute, the largest ever for Mars, will slow the rover during its 2028 landing to search for signs of life.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for capturing and securing objects in space. This innovation could revolutionize satellite servicing and debris removal missions.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has successfully completed space-like tests, remaining on track for a 2027 launch to search for terrestrial planets.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA invites SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual consultation process, including an SME Forum in June 2026 at ESA Headquarters in Paris, preceded by online sessions in April and May.

title: Astrolab Unveils Payloads Flying on FLIP Lunar Mission
summary: Astrolab has announced the payloads that will fly on its upcoming FLIP lunar mission, scheduled for this year. The mission will carry a variety of scientific payloads onboard a lunar rover.

title: Potomac Database Systems Unveils Plans to Amass Lunar Data
summary: Potomac Database Systems has announced plans to collect and manage lunar data, highlighting commercial opportunities beyond traditional lunar mining and infrastructure. The initiative aims to capitalize on the growing interest in lunar exploration and utilization.

title: U-Space Selects Skynopy to Support Multiple Missions
summary: U-Space has selected Skynopy to support multiple missions using its ground-station-as-a-service model. Skynopy's service is gaining significant traction in the space industry.

title: Intuitive Machines Bets on Lunar Comms with Goonhilly Buy
summary: Intuitive Machines acquired UK-based Goonhilly Earth Station and its US subsidiary COMSAT to bolster its lunar communications and PNT services. The move aims to provide customers with an integrated deep-space communications solution.

title: Iridium Boosts Air Safety Offering With Aireon Acquisition
summary: Iridium has acquired Aireon to enhance its air safety services by integrating space-based VHF communications. This move aims to strengthen Iridium's offerings in aviation safety and global air traffic management.

title: European Space Startups Jockey for ‘Infrastructure’ Financing
summary: European space startups are seeking infrastructure financing to bridge a growth-stage-capital gap. The challenge highlights Europe's struggle to compete with larger space economies.

title: Guetlein: CBO is ‘Not Estimating What We’re Building’
summary: The head of the Golden Dome program criticized the Congressional Budget Office's $1.2 trillion cost estimate for the missile-defense program, stating it does not accurately reflect what is being built. The program's leader argued that the CBO's estimation methods are flawed.

title: Meet Blacknight Space Labs In-Orbit Infrastructure Accelerator
summary: Blacknight Space Labs is launching an In-Orbit Infrastructure Accelerator offering up to $100,000 per company, along with mentorship and support for entering the defense space market.

title: NASA Lays Out Preliminary Artemis III Mission Plan
summary: NASA has outlined the preliminary plan for the Artemis III mission, targeting a 2027 launch to test technologies needed for a Moon landing in 2028. The mission will involve astronauts traveling to Low Earth Orbit (LEO) to validate critical capabilities.

title: Exclusive: LEAP Launches its First Rocket
summary: LEAP successfully launched its first rocket, marking a significant milestone for the company. This event introduces a new player in the space launch industry.

title: Lynk and Anterix Target D2D for Critical Infrastructure In New Testing
summary: Lynk Global and Anterix are collaborating to test direct-to-device (D2D) communications for critical infrastructure sectors. The partnership aims to enable private D2D networks for various use cases.

title: Skynopy to Support U-Space Missions With Ground Station Services
summary: Skynopy has secured a contract with Toulouse-based U-Space to provide ground station services for two of its small satellites. This partnership will support U-Space's next-generation satellite operations.

title: Space Force Awards Northrop Grumman $398 Million For Enhanced PTS-P
summary: The U.S. Space Force awarded Northrop Grumman a $398 million contract to develop and build an Enhanced Protected Tactical Satellite Communications-Prototype (PTS-P). This contract aims to enhance secure satellite communications capabilities for military operations.

title: iDirectGov Releases WCore Virtualization Tool, Supporting 16 Waveforms
summary: iDirect Government has launched WCore, a new virtualization tool supporting 16 waveforms for rapid deployment, scaling, and management. The tool aims to enhance flexibility and efficiency in satellite communications.

title: What Will the Golden Dome Cost? Gen. Guetlein Pushes Back on CBO Cost Estimate
summary: Gen. Guetlein, head of the Golden Dome program, disputed a Congressional Budget Office (CBO) cost estimate for the system. The disagreement highlights concerns over the program's budget and feasibility.

title: IFC Market in China and India is On Cusp of Growth, Valour Predicts
summary: Valour Consultancy predicts significant growth in the In-Flight Connectivity (IFC) market for China and India. The consultancy's latest report highlights the potential scale of these emerging markets.

title: Cowboy Space Files for 20,000-Satellite Constellation for Data Center Services
summary: Cowboy Space has filed for a 20,000-satellite constellation to provide data center services from space, following a $275 million Series B funding round. The company, previously rebranded, aims to leverage this massive satellite network for space-based data center operations.

title: Iceye Delivers SAR Capability to Polish Armed Forces
summary: Iceye has delivered control over a radar satellite constellation to the Polish Armed Forces for sovereign intelligence within 12 months. This marks a significant milestone in Poland's space capabilities.

title: Kymeta Terminals to be Integrated Into Red Cat Uncrewed Surface Vessels
summary: Kymeta is partnering with Red Cat Futures to integrate its satcom terminals into uncrewed surface vessels. The collaboration was announced on May 14.

title: Major US Carries Band Together in Joint Venture to Encourage D2D Competition
summary: The three major U.S. mobile carriers—AT&T, T-Mobile, and Verizon—have formed a joint venture to streamline direct-to-device (D2D) services for customers. This collaboration aims to enhance competition in the D2D space.
